title: Time-varying discrete hazard with monotone wear effects
summary: The strongest adjacent statistical framing is still discrete-time survival, but the source material suggests the effect of tyre age is not constant: undercut power, warm-up penalty, and degradation all vary by race phase and track context.
rationale: The strongest adjacent statistical framing is still discrete-time survival, but the source material suggests the effect of tyre age is not constant: undercut power, warm-up penalty, and degradation all vary by race phase and track context. A hazard model with varying coefficients is a more specific next step than a generic multi-horizon setup.
implementation_hint: Expand each current row into a next-lap hazard target as before, but fit a complementary log-log or logistic discrete hazard model with spline or GAM-style interactions for `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `LapsRemaining`, allowing coefficients to vary with `RaceProgress`, `Compound`, and inferred regime. Enforce monotonicity where sensible on wear-related terms, then ensemble with the tree model rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Should sharpen probability shape around the true pit window and reduce ad hoc threshold-feature dependence.
risk: This partially overlaps with prior hazard work; gains depend on the varying-coefficient piece being genuinely new and validated with strict race-blocked OOF.
